title: Neuburgia sarcantha
intro: Neuburgia sarcantha is a plant species belonging to the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities. The bioactive constituents of Neuburgia sarcantha include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, including neuburgine and other related alkaloids that are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Neuburgia sarcantha include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory infections, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Neuburgia sarcantha is a species of flowering plant in the family Menispermaceae. It belongs to the genus Neuburgia, which consists of a small group of tropical plants native to the Pacific Islands. The plant is an evergreen climber that thrives in humid environments. Neuburgia sarcantha is classified under the kingdom Plantae, and its classification is further divided into the phylum Magnoliophyta and the class Magnoliopsida. Within its class, it is placed in the order Ranunculales.
